name: Roasted Cherry Tomato Basil Pasta
author: Henry
why: When you've got genuinely good cherry tomatoes, roasting them is the move. The oven concentrates their sweetness, slightly chars the skins, and turns them into something almost jammy — way deeper than just tossing them raw into a pan. It's a 45-minute summer dinner that tastes like it took hours.
source: Inspired by It's Not Complicated Recipes

steps: Wash and dry the cherry tomatoes thoroughly. Cut them in half.
Mince the garlic.
Roughly tear half the basil leaves.
Preheat oven to 200°C / 400°F.
Toss the halved tomatoes in a roasting dish with olive oil, minced garlic, half the basil, dried oregano, sugar, salt, and pepper. Spread them in a single layer.
Roast for 30–40 minutes until the tomatoes are collapsing, slightly charred at the edges, and releasing their juices.
While tomatoes roast, bring a large pot of well-salted water to the boil. Cook pasta until al dente.
Reserve a cup of pasta water before draining.
Drain the pasta and add it directly to the roasting dish with the tomatoes — don't wash the dish, those roasted bits are gold.
Toss everything together. Add a splash of pasta water if it needs loosening.
Stir in the grated Parmesan.
Scatter the remaining fresh basil over the top.
Crack black pepper generously and add red pepper flakes if you want heat.
Finish with a final drizzle of olive oil.

pro_tips: The sugar isn't just for sweetness — it accelerates caramelization and helps the tomatoes collapse into that jammy texture. If your tomatoes are already super sweet, you can skip it.
Don't skip the pasta water. The starch is what emulsifies the olive oil and tomato juices into a proper sauce that clings to the pasta instead of pooling at the bottom of the plate.

name: Pizza Dipping Sauces Collection
author: Henry
why: A great dipping sauce elevates homemade pizza from nice to proper thing. These range from classic to adventurous — all quick to make, all pair brilliantly with pizza. Pick one (or three) and keep them in a ramekin on the side.

steps: Crush 2 cloves garlic and mix with 4 tbsp mayonnaise, 1 tsp lemon juice, and a splash of olive oil.
Whisk everything together. Done in 2 minutes. Best with: everything — the universal dip.
Blend or finely chop: 1 large handful fresh parsley, 2 cloves garlic, 2 tbsp red wine vinegar, 80ml olive oil, 1 tsp chilli flakes, pinch of salt.
Let it sit 10 minutes for flavours to meld. Best with: sausage pizza, fennel, lemon.
Mix 3 tbsp honey with 1-2 tsp chilli flakes. Let sit 10 minutes.
Drizzle over pizza or dip. Add more flakes if you want it hotter. Best with: Margherita, anything cheesy.
Blend: large handful fresh basil, 30g pine nuts (or walnuts), 1 clove garlic, 40g grated parmesan, 60ml olive oil.
Blend until smooth, drizzle oil in slowly. Season with salt. Best with: white pizza, burrata, mozzarella-heavy.
Cook down: 400g tin chopped tomatoes, 3 cloves crushed garlic, 1 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp sugar, fresh basil, salt and black pepper.
Cook 20+ minutes until thick and intense. More garlic than you think. Cool before dipping. Best with: any pizza.
Whisk together: 100g Greek yogurt, 1-2 tbsp harissa paste, 1 tsp lemon juice.
Creamy with a slow-building heat. Surprisingly brilliant with pizza. Best with: spiced toppings, Middle Eastern twist.
Mix: 4 tbsp mayonnaise, 1 tsp truffle oil, 1 clove crushed garlic.
Three ingredients. Fancy but effortless. Best with: mushrooms, truffle oil pizza.
Use good quality balsamic vinegar reduction — buy it or reduce balsamic vinegar in a pan until syrupy.
Thick, sweet-tart. Best with: burrata, fresh tomatoes, anything cheesy.
Mix: 3 tbsp Frank's RedHot (or hot sauce), 2 tbsp melted butter, splash of white vinegar.
Classic American heat. Best with: chicken pizza, American-style.
Whisk: 4 tbsp mayonnaise, 1-2 tbsp sriracha, squeeze of lime.
Adjust sriracha to your heat preference. Best with: Asian-inspired toppings.

pro_tips: Make 2-3 sauces at once and keep them in small ramekins on the table for a proper pizza night experience.
Chimichurri and pesto both keep well in the fridge for a week — make extra.
Hot honey doubles as a drizzle over the pizza itself, not just a dip.
The marinara dip should be cooked longer and thicker than what you'd put on pizza — it needs to stand up to repeated dipping.

name: Seared Salmon with Avocado-Lime Salsa, Warm Spinach & Chilli Beans
author: Henry
why: Vietnamese-inspired plate that's bright, fresh, and layered with gentle heat. Uses seasonal avocado and radish with crispy-skinned salmon — restaurant quality in 25 minutes.
ingredients: 200g fine beans, trimmed, cut into 4cm lengths
2 spring onions, white parts only, thinly sliced
½ lime, cut into wedges for serving

steps: Pat salmon dry, season generously with salt and pepper.
Dice avocados, toss immediately with lime juice to prevent browning.
Slice radishes into thin rounds.
Slice both chillies — rocket chilli rings for the beans, red chilli strips for the salsa.
Chop herbs, toast sesame seeds in a dry pan for 1 minute until fragrant.
If using mango, peel and dice.
Heat oil and butter in a large pan over medium-high.
Place salmon skin-side down, press gently for 10 seconds so the skin lays flat.
Cook 4-5 minutes until skin is crispy, flip, cook 2-3 minutes more.
Remove to a plate, tent loosely with foil, rest.
In the same pan (keep the salmon fond!), add olive oil.
Sauté white spring onion parts and garlic for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add both chillies, cook 30 seconds more — the heat blooms into the oil.
Toss in fine beans, cook 2-3 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add fish sauce, season with salt and pepper.
Toss in baby spinach, cook just until wilted (30 seconds).
Remove from heat.
In a bowl, combine diced avocado, radish slices, mint, coriander, and mango if using.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and sea salt flakes.
Dress with lime juice and lemon juice, fold gently — don't mash the avocado.
Spoon warm spinach and beans as the base.
Place salmon fillet on top.
Ladle salsa generously over everything.
Scatter cheese — mozzarella will soften from the heat, feta or goat cheese will add tang.
Final squeeze of lime, extra rocket chilli rings if you want more heat.

pro_tips: Press the salmon gently for 10 seconds when it hits the pan — prevents curling and ensures even searing.
Bloom the chillies in hot oil before adding beans — it distributes heat evenly rather than in hot spots.
Dress avocado with acid immediately after cutting to stop oxidation.
If mozzarella feels too mild, swap for feta or goat cheese for a sharper tang that cuts through the richness.
Mango is optional but highly recommended — the sweetness balances the chilli heat beautifully.
Don't overcook the spinach — 30 seconds is enough. It'll keep wilting from residual heat.
